results.title: Overflight Permits – Part 1: General Requirements
results.content:
In most cases, you cannot operate directly between Taiwan and China, or between Argentina and the Falkland Islands.
 Israel is not available for overflight.
 If you’re operating an Israeli-built aircraft, you will not be permitted to overfly most countries in the Middle East.
 When operating between China and India, you’re pretty much restricted to the PURPA route – which may be available with sufficient advance notification. Keep in mind, however, that if China denies this request, this means adding additional time to your routing and having to revise both routes and permits.
 Venezuela CAA will not let you overfly the country if you have outstanding overflight fees, and they may not let you know this until you’re close to entering their FIR. [...] ## 1. Overflight permit requirements

Many countries impose overflight permit requirements, which apply to both private non-revenue and charter (non-scheduled commercial) flight activity. Over recent years more countries have been adding such requirements. Suriname and Brazil for example did not have overflight permit requirements in the past, but they both now do. For aircraft flying on experimental or non-full airworthiness certificates, overflight permits are generally required even if the country in question does not require a permit for standard General Aviation (GA) flights. Experimental aircraft generally face longer permit lead times, additional documentation, and specific aircraft operating restrictions.

## 2. Overflight permits are not always required [...] Universal Weather and Aviation

# Overflight Permits – Part 1: General Requirements

Bizav Best Practice

By Shawn Leavell

January 6, 2015

PT  3 M minute read

8912

Share:

This business aviation blog post is part of a series on overflight permits.

Overflight permits are authorizations from Civil Aviation Authorities (CAAs) to overfly that country’s airspace. These differ in terms of regulations and airspace coverage area, depending on the country. It’s always important to check overflight permit requirements in advance with your 3rd-party provider for the planned route of flight.

The following is an overview of what you need to know:

## 1. Overflight permit requirements

results.title: Canada did not shut down airspace to US carriers | Andre Vermette posted on the topic | LinkedIn
results.content:
Here’s a perfect example of a fake news story that claims Mark Carney has shut down Canadian air space to American carriers. The core claim — that Canada suspended preferential Arctic overflight rights for U.S. carriers via a “quiet administrative airspace notice” — has no credible reporting to support it. There is no evidence Canada has blocked or restricted American commercial flights from using Canadian airspace in 2026. Here’s what’s actually happening in Canada-U.S. aviation right now, which may have inspired or been conflated with this claim: Canadian airlines like WestJet, Air Transat, and Air Canada are voluntarily pulling back on U.S. routes and shifting capacity toward Mexico, driven by reduced Canadian traveler demand following Trump-era tariffs and political tensions — not any [...] Trump-era tariffs and political tensions — not any government airspace order. These are commercial decisions, not sovereign airspace actions. Framing these reduced services as a political “block” conflates private-sector route decisions and U.S. regulatory actions with a sovereign Canadian interdiction. The flight disruptions that have been widely reported stem from FAA actions and market-driven route cuts, not a Canadian ban on U.S. overflights. The post is well-written and makes a plausible-sounding geopolitical argument, but it appears to be built on a fictional premise. The “quiet administrative airspace notice” it references has not been reported by any credible aviation or news source. The rhetorical framing (“Not with a press conference… through a quiet administrative notice”) is a [...] Image 41: No alternative text description for this image

results.title: Options Basics
results.content:
User acknowledges review of the Terms and Conditions and Privacy Policy governing this site. Continued use constitutes acceptance of the terms and conditions stated therein. [...] The opposite of a call option is a put option, which gives its holder the right to sell 100 shares of the underlying security at the strike price, anytime before the option's expiration date. The writer (or seller) of the option has the obligation to buy the shares.

|  |  |  |
 --- 
|  | Holder (Buyer) | Writer (Seller) |
| Call Option | Right to buy | Obligation to sell |
| Put Option | Right to sell | Obligation to buy |

### The Options Premium

An option's price is called the premium and is priced at a per share basis. The option holder’s potential loss is limited to the initial premium paid for the contract. Alternately, the writer collects the premium and has theoretically unlimited loss potential for a call option and substantial loss potential for a put option.
